data.title: CGBIO Showcases Comprehensive Medical Device Portfolio at KIMES 2026 Achieves KRW 5.3 Billion in Contract Results
data.description: CGBIO, based in Seoul, South Korea, recently showcased various product lines in areas such as aesthetics, wound care, bone and spine, and surgery. They have successfully secured supply contracts amounting to KRW 5.3 billion with partners in Singapore, Malaysia, Pakistan, Taiwan, and the Philippines.

data.title: Inc. Names CFO Worx to Its 2026 List of the Fastest-Growing Private Companies in the Southeast
data.description: In Tampa, Florida, CFO Worx has secured the 19th spot on the Inc. Regionals: Southeast list, a renowned ranking of rapidly expanding private companies in the Southeastern region, encompassing South Carolina, Kentucky, Tennessee, Georgia, Arkansas, Alabama, Mississippi, Louisiana, Florida, and Puerto Rico. This list, by the leading entrepreneurial media brand Inc., showcases the top performers driving innovation and growth in the area.

data.title: ReelTime's Annual Financials Reveal Major Capital Structure Improvements Designed to Protect Shareholder Value and Reduce Potential Dilution
data.description: Approximately 685 million potential shares have been diminished or removed, maintaining a stable share count since July 2025. ReelTime emphasizes debt reduction, disciplined dilution, and capital management in an artificial intelligence market dominated by Microsoft (MSFT), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Alphabet (GOOGL).

data.title: IRS Data Shows Average Tax Refunds Jump 10.9% In 2026 Filing Season
data.description: The latest information from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) indicates that the average tax refund in the United States for the 2026 filing season has seen an increase, reaching $3,571 by March 20. This marks a $350 rise, or 10.9%, from the previous year's average of $3,221 at the same period. Total refund payments have also gone up, with the IRS issuing over $202 billion compared to $179 billion in the prior year. The number of refunds has slightly increased to approximately 56.7 million, showing a 1.8% rise. Despite the higher refund amounts, there has been a slight slowdown in overall filing activity. Total returns received decreased by 0.9% to 78.9 million, and returns processed fell by 1.1% to 77.8 million. More individuals are opting to file on their own, with self-prepared returns increasing by 1.9% to surpass 37.8 million. In contrast, filings handled by tax professionals dropped by 1% to 39.7 million.

data.title: Jerome Powell Sounds Alarm On $39 Trillion 'Unsustainable' Debt Path, Says It Will 'Not End Well'
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell raised concerns about the U.S. fiscal trajectory at Harvard University, mentioning the $39 trillion debt load. Powell emphasized that while not an immediate threat, the debt is unsustainable and requires urgent legislative action. He warned that if nothing is done soon, the outcome will be unfavorable. Powell highlighted the distinction between the level of debt and its trajectory, noting the U.S.' unique position as the world's reserve currency issuer and having deep capital markets allowing it to sustain a larger debt load than smaller economies. When asked about the debt's breaking point, Powell stated the exact threshold is uncertain. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Alderbuck Energy Welcomes Ajith Wijenayake as Chief Technology Officer
data.description: In San Diego, Alderbuck Energy has appointed Ajith Wijenayake as Chief Technology Officer, bringing over 30 years of expertise in power electronics, electrification, and energy storage to the company. With a comprehensive background in the evolution of the power electronics sector, from initial motor drive innovations to the most recent SiC semiconductor advancements, Ajith's experience aligns well with Alderbuck's strategic initiatives. His appointment underscores Alderbuck's commitment to innovation and growth in the industry.

data.title: AHF on World Health Day: No Equity, No Pandemic Deal
data.description: On April 7, World Health Day, AIDS Healthcare Foundation (AHF) is urging global solidarity in the fight against infectious diseases. The organization is calling on key global leaders, particularly in Europe, to support an equitable World Health Organization Pandemic Agreement, which includes a binding Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing (PABS) Annex. AHF emphasizes that without equity, a Pandemic Agreement is not possible. This call for action coincides with a crucial moment as the world faces a heightened need for international cooperation to combat health crises.
